content:
Hi <@1376763322568937562>,

I had two questions regarding the updates and approval process

1. The existing source sheet is slightly outdated as it does not include any text from Kathigu-ra's event, or her character profile and card details. It seems like the source sheet comes from a previous patch, as it does not include any of the changes made to character skills and passives in the most recent update either.

Can I ask what the plan is for updating the source sheet? It would be bad if we were to edit a character that has been changed in a recent patch, as it would revert the ingame text for their kit back to what it was before the update. Additionally, it would be good if source sheet updates would include the changes that we made as well once they get pushed to live, so that we don't end up overwriting our own changes accidentally.

2. What is the process and timeline for approving updates to the "EN-Morimens Term Base" spreadsheet in the "Suggested Terms" column? I have been adding some suggested terms as I go, so it would be nice to have an approval or a veto for each of the terms so that I know whether to leave these term changes in the text, or to revert it back to what it was before.

Adding to this :
1. it would be very nice if the csv conversion was done by BIAV and then just we had the full files slapped on github. This would mean resolving conflicts in merges, cherry-picking modifications & generally having atomic commits would go way smoother.
2. There's a github integration for discord, we could have a channel here with a feed of the pull requests & comments, I think that would be kinda neat.

is this sheet a part of Gameplay sheet of the source sheet? KR team were prioritizing UI part, but I found most of them shares the same keys in gameplay sheet. When creating a pull request, should we edit the "source sheet" part only, and ui&misc sheet only as reference?
also, what's the difference between the keys "LauguageConfig" and "PanelText"? I'm assuming paneltexts are more direct UI-related ones
some of them don't even match keys
located all matching part in gameplay tab, we will edit only gameplay sheet, using UI&Misc sheet only for reference, for now

Here's some excerpts from a lengthy discussion that was had in <#1402538040253087766>, I'd love if you could weigh in, <@1376763322568937562>. <:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541> 

Mercy:
> I'd like to discuss the inconsistency of the terms used to describe the menu in which you select the 4 Awakeners that will accompany you into an exploration, and the terms used to describe said conglomerate of Awakeners.
> The menu is called **Lineup**. Wheels and Covenants describe some effects as **Team** Unique. The button to quick-select Awakeners says Quick **Team Lineup**. The default name for each of the 10 setups you can have is **Party**(N). The menu to see collective stats is called **Team** Information. Once in an exploration, any collectively applied buffs are described using a random mix of **Team**, **You**, **All Awakeners**, or simply with no pronoun whatsoever. ("Crit Rate +50%").
> 
> If it were up to me, I'd go the "let's get fancy" route and standardize all of these descriptions to **Entourage**. Why? First, it sounds cool as hell. Style points, yo. Second, it does a great job of reflecting the diagetic situation, being that the Awakeners have more of a compositional connection to the Keeper than to the other members of their "team".
> 
> If **Entourage** is too much of a mouthful though, my second pick was **Troupe**, but at that point I'd rather just default back to **Team**.

Lens:
> i prefer team/party since I think basic gaming terminology should be consistent and simple to understand
> fancier terms should be used for unique mechanics but the idea of a party is fairly standard across games

Mercy:
> I refute your point by agreeing with it. The fact that the idea of a team or party is so blatantly standard across all games means that we have some space to make the game feel *less basic.* 
> And if we make it clear at all points, in all menus, that the word we're using is Entourage, and make new players "assemble an entourage" during the tutorial, then players will understand what it means when a Relic gives you "`Entourage Crit Rate +50%`."

Adelle:
> joining in.. 
> I would give 1 vote for entourage. the reason?, first, it gives off different vibes compared to another similar game. second, it vibes with the style of the games lore in the way that word refers to .. well.. group of special people (according to my dictionary).. so, +1 from me for Entourage

Dredge:
> *(...)*so I am 100% down for this unless Ace says otherwise

Lens:
> I lean conservative in translation philosophy so I remain against it
> I think it's important to pay attention to what changes need to be made for the sake of clarity, and what changes you want to make *for the sake of making changes*
> *(...)*
> please keep in mind that we are here to communicate the dev's intentions into a different language as accurately as possible
> __our job is not to assign intention or to create meaning where there is none__
> the devs have a vision for their game, and our role as translators is to convey the voice of the original work as accurately as possible
> that said I really do wish we had a direct link to either a dev or a narrative designer because it's certainly one of those things where I could see them wanting it
> but without active confirmation I always lean conservative in terms of changes made

Mercy:
> You are, by all means and measures, absolutely correct.
> ## My argument is, however, that if the original language of a work did not use or does not have a word available to them that conveys the mood of the work as well as what another language may have, is it better to keep the less-interesting word for the sake of transliteration?
> *(...)*
> the real question at hand here, because it was never made terribly clear (at least to me), is whether our job is Transliteration, Translation, or Localization.
> Seeing as the game has already been fully transliterated into English (by AI), I was under the assumption that our job was localization.
